strengths: 15+ years of hands-on engineering across distributed systems, high-throughput microservices (millions of req/s at Sociomantic), and event-driven architectures directly relevant to the fintech infrastructure stack
Current exposure to agentic AI tooling and MCP framework development at re:cinq provides a foothold in the domain, showing proactive alignment with the role's core focus

Consistent record of working across regulated and commercially consequential environments (RIDE Capital, Optilyz pricing systems) with strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No engineering leadership or management experience anywhere in the 15-year career — all roles are individual contributor positions; the role requires scaling teams from 10 to 30+ and building a senior leadership layer

AI/agentic work is surface-level and recent — described as 'contributing to' infrastructure rather than owning architecture of production multi-agent systems; no evidence of LLM evaluation methodology, fallback strategies, or cost/latency modeling at scale

No substantive fintech infrastructure experience — RIDE Capital was a brief 15-month IC stint; no demonstrated work with credit systems, real-time risk, working-capital allocation, payment flows, or compliance across jurisdictions

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and high-throughput backend experience

response.justification: Bogdan is a technically strong, broadly experienced individual contributor with real distributed-systems credibility and emerging AI tooling exposure, but the gap between his profile and this CTO role is structural rather than incremental: the role requires executive-level engineering leadership, a proven track record of scaling and developing engineering organizations, and demonstrated ownership of production multi-agent AI systems in high-stakes environments — none of which appear in his history. His current work at re:cinq touches agentic workflows but as a contributor, not an architect or leader, and his fintech exposure is too narrow to satisfy the credit-risk and working-capital domain requirements. He would be a strong senior or staff engineer candidate, but the seniority and domain mismatch for this executive role is significant.
